title: Kubernetes Fundamentals
description: Learn Kubernetes from the ground up. Starting with container basics and cluster architecture, you will progress through pods, deployments, services, networking, storage, and security. By the end, you will be comfortable deploying and managing applications on Kubernetes and troubleshooting common issues.

modules.title: Container Basics Recap
modules.description: Review container fundamentals: images, registries, Dockerfiles, and container runtimes. Establishes the foundation for understanding why orchestration is needed.
objectives: Explain the difference between containers and virtual machines
Build and run a container from a Dockerfile
Push and pull images from a container registry

modules.title: Kubernetes Architecture
modules.description: Understand the control plane and worker node components: API server, etcd, scheduler, controller manager, kubelet, and kube-proxy.
objectives: Identify the role of each control plane component
Describe how worker nodes communicate with the control plane
Explain the declarative model and reconciliation loop

modules.title: Setting Up kubectl and a Local Cluster
modules.description: Install kubectl, configure kubeconfig, and spin up a local cluster with kind or minikube for hands-on practice.
objectives: Install and configure kubectl
Create a local Kubernetes cluster using kind or minikube
Verify cluster health with kubectl commands

modules.title: Pods - The Smallest Deployable Unit
modules.description: Deep dive into pods: single-container and multi-container patterns, pod lifecycle, resource requests and limits, and writing pod manifests.
objectives: Define what a Pod is and why it exists
Write a Pod YAML manifest from scratch
Configure resource requests and limits
Inspect and debug running pods with kubectl

modules.title: Deployments and ReplicaSets
modules.description: Learn how Deployments manage ReplicaSets to provide declarative updates, rolling rollouts, and rollbacks for your applications.
objectives: Create a Deployment and understand its relationship to ReplicaSets
Perform rolling updates and rollbacks
Scale a Deployment horizontally
Use deployment strategies (RollingUpdate vs Recreate)

modules.title: Services and Networking Basics
modules.description: Expose pods to each other and to the outside world using ClusterIP, NodePort, and LoadBalancer services. Understand Kubernetes DNS and service discovery.
objectives: Explain the three main Service types and when to use each
Create a Service to expose a Deployment
Describe how Kubernetes DNS enables service discovery
Debug connectivity issues between services

modules.title: ConfigMaps and Secrets
modules.description: Externalize application configuration using ConfigMaps and manage sensitive data with Secrets. Mount them as environment variables or volumes.
objectives: Create ConfigMaps from literals, files, and directories
Inject ConfigMap data as environment variables and volume mounts
Create and consume Secrets safely
Understand the limitations of Kubernetes Secrets

modules.title: Namespaces and Resource Organization
modules.description: Organize cluster resources with namespaces. Apply resource quotas and limit ranges to enforce multi-tenancy guardrails.
objectives: Create and switch between namespaces
Apply ResourceQuotas to limit namespace consumption
Set LimitRanges for default resource constraints
Understand cross-namespace communication

modules.title: Ingress and External Access
modules.description: Route external HTTP/S traffic to services using Ingress resources. Configure host-based and path-based routing with an Ingress controller.
objectives: Install and configure an Ingress controller (e.g., NGINX)
Write Ingress rules for host-based and path-based routing
Configure TLS termination on Ingress
Troubleshoot Ingress misconfigurations

modules.title: Persistent Storage
modules.description: Manage stateful workloads with PersistentVolumes, PersistentVolumeClaims, and StorageClasses. Understand static and dynamic provisioning.
objectives: Explain the PV, PVC, and StorageClass relationship
Create a PersistentVolumeClaim and mount it in a Pod
Configure dynamic provisioning with a StorageClass
Understand access modes and reclaim policies

modules.title: RBAC and Security Basics
modules.description: Secure your cluster with Role-Based Access Control. Create Roles, ClusterRoles, RoleBindings, and ClusterRoleBindings. Understand ServiceAccounts.
objectives: Explain the RBAC authorization model in Kubernetes
Create Roles and RoleBindings for namespace-scoped access
Create ClusterRoles for cluster-wide permissions
Assign ServiceAccounts to pods for API access

modules.title: Helm - Package Management for Kubernetes
modules.description: Use Helm to package, template, and deploy applications. Work with charts, values files, and the Helm release lifecycle.
objectives: Install Helm and add chart repositories
Deploy an application using a Helm chart
Customize deployments with values files and overrides
Upgrade and rollback Helm releases

modules.title: Troubleshooting Kubernetes
modules.description: Develop a systematic approach to diagnosing and fixing common Kubernetes issues: CrashLoopBackOff, ImagePullBackOff, pending pods, and networking failures.
objectives: Use kubectl describe, logs, and events to diagnose issues
Troubleshoot pod scheduling failures
Debug container crash loops and image pull errors
Diagnose service and networking connectivity problems

modules.title: Capstone: Deploy a Multi-Tier Application
modules.description: Bring it all together by deploying a multi-tier web application with a frontend, backend API, and database, complete with services, configmaps, secrets, ingress, and persistent storage.
objectives: Design Kubernetes manifests for a multi-tier application
Configure inter-service communication and external access
Manage configuration and secrets across tiers
Verify the full deployment end-to-end
